visa_free.notes: EU citizens have the right to free movement and residence under EU Directive 2004/38/EC. No visa or immigration check applies at internal Schengen borders. Unlimited stay rights. Exempt from EES procedure.

visa_free.nationality: Schengen Area nationals (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland)
visa_free.notes: Free movement under bilateral and EEA/Schengen agreements. No visa required. Exempt from EES procedure.
Visa-free per EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II. Nationals may also stay in France beyond 90 days. Exempt from EES procedure.

Visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period per EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (post-Brexit). UK citizens (British citizens) travel visa-free; certain other categories of British nationals are listed in Annex II Part 3 (British Nationals Overseas, British Overseas Territories Citizens, British Overseas Citizens, British Protected Persons, British Subjects).

Visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period per EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II. France also has a bilateral agreement with the USA since 1 April 1949 specifically allowing US citizens 90 days in metropolitan France. Note: US diplomatic and service passport holders travelling for professional reasons require a visa (Annexe C of Arrêté du 10 mai 2010).
Visa-free for up to 90 days in any 180-day period per EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II.

eta.nationality: All visa-free Annex II nationals (approx. 61 countries/territories)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be required for all Annex II visa-exempt nationals for Schengen travel. EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026. ETIAS is expected to launch in the last quarter of 2026 (exact date to be announced). Cost approx. EUR 7. Valid for 3 years or until passport expiry, multiple entries, max 90 days per 180-day period. EU/EEA/Schengen and micro-state nationals (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino, Vatican) remain exempt from both EES and ETIAS.

visa_policy.notes: France applies the common Schengen visa policy as mandated by EU Regulation 2018/1806. Visa-free nationals from Annex II are allowed stays up to 90 days in any 180-day period. EU/EEA/Schengen and micro-state nationals (Andorra, Monaco, San Marino, Vatican) are exempt from both the EES border system and future ETIAS requirement. The EES (Entry/Exit System) became fully operational across all Schengen countries on 10 April 2026 for non-EU/Schengen nationals. ETIAS authorization for visa-free Annex II nationals is expected to launch in late 2026 (exact date to be announced). France has no visa-on-arrival or e-visa schemes for the Schengen zone — visitors either travel visa-free or must obtain a Schengen visa in advance. Vanuatu was permanently removed from Annex II and transferred to Annex I (visa required) via Regulation (EU) 2025/11 effective 3 February 2025. United Kingdom, Hong Kong SAR, and Macao SAR are included in the current (3 February 2025) consolidated Annex II. Timor-Leste is confirmed visa-free with EU agreement in force since 1 May 2022. Nauru remains in Annex II with a footnote making the exemption conditional on an EU-Nauru visa waiver agreement that has not yet been concluded.

credential.subtype: carte de résident de longue durée-UE (EU long-term residence permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid EU long-term residence card (carte de résident de longue durée-UE) issued by another EU member state (Directive 2003/109/CE), plus eligible family members as defined in Article 2(2) of Directive 2004/38. Exempts holder from requiring a long-stay visa to cross into France. The holder must satisfy general entry conditions (valid travel document, sufficient resources, no SIS alert). Subject to Articles L.313-4-1 and L.313-11-1 of the French Code on Entry and Stay of Foreigners.

credential.subtype: titre de séjour ICT (intra-corporate transferee permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Holder of a valid residence permit bearing the mention 'ICT' issued by another EU member state, allowing intra-EU mobility under Directive 2014/66/UE. If the permit is issued by a non-Schengen EU state, the holder must also present a letter from the host company in France specifying the duration of the mobility and the company address. Family members covered under Directive 2003/86/CE Article 4(1). References Articles L.313-7-2 and L.313-24 of the French CESEDA.

cbi.notes: France does not offer a citizenship-by-investment (CBI) program. There is no direct purchase of French nationality. Citizenship can only be obtained after a minimum of 5 years of lawful regular residence (reduced in some circumstances), meeting language requirements (B2 level French from 1 January 2026), and civic examination. Investor residence through the Talent Passport may eventually lead to naturalization but is not a CBI program.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Economic Investor (Passeport Talent - Investisseur economique)
programs.notes: Requires a direct economic investment of at least EUR 300,000 in tangible or intangible fixed assets. Must create or maintain jobs within four years of investment. Investment may be through personal ownership, a managed company, or a 30%+ capital stake. Pure financial investments excluded. Permit valid up to 4 years, renewable. After 5 years of continuous regular residence, eligible for 10-year permanent residence card permitting any professional activity. Administrative fees: EUR 300 + EUR 50 stamp duty + EUR 99 long-stay visa fee. Confirmed current as of May 2026 via Business France.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Investor
programs.notes: Dedicated fast-track procedure within the Talent Passport framework for foreign investors in the French Tech/startup ecosystem. Requires minimum EUR 300,000 direct investment and commitment to create/preserve jobs within 4 years. Handled by the French Tech Mission on a one-to-one basis. Results in 'Talent - Business Investor' residence permit valid up to 4 years, renewable.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Qualified Employee (Passeport Talent - Salarie qualifie)
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals with a Master's degree (or equivalent) and employment contract in France with annual gross salary of at least EUR 39,582 (as of August 2025). Also covers employees of innovative companies (JEI) and intra-company transferees with 3 months seniority.
programs.processing_time: 4 months (implicit refusal if no response within 4 months)
programs.notes: Multi-year residence card valid up to 4 years. No separate work authorization required. Family members (spouse and children) eligible for 'Passeport Talent Famille' permit. Administrative cost: EUR 350 (EUR 300 tax + EUR 50 stamp duty). Applications submitted fully online via administration-etrangers-en-france.interieur.gouv.fr.

programs.name: Talent Passport - European Blue Card
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with at least 3 years of higher education and employment contract with annual gross salary of at least EUR 59,373.
programs.processing_time: 30 days for EU Blue Card holders from other EU states; 4 months for new applicants
programs.notes: EU Blue Card category within the Talent Passport framework. Higher salary threshold than standard qualified employee category. Valid up to 4 years.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Researcher
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with a Master's degree plus a hosting agreement with a French research institution. Minimum resources: EUR 2,200/month.
programs.notes: Faster processing (60 days) compared to other Talent Passport categories. Valid for the duration of the hosting agreement up to 4 years.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Founders
programs.eligibility: Non-EU/EEA/Swiss nationals developing an innovative project in France, recognized as innovative by a public organization (DRIEETS). Minimum financial resources equivalent to minimum wage (SMIC). Must be selected by a partner incubator/accelerator for the expedited French Tech Visa pathway.
programs.processing_time: Not specified; standard Talent Passport timelines apply
programs.notes: Issues 'Talent - Innovative Project' residence permit valid up to 4 years. Family eligible for accompanying status with work rights. After 5 years of regular residence, eligible for permanent residence. From 1 January 2026, B2 French language level required for naturalization.

programs.name: French Tech Visa - Employees
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals recruited by innovative startups in France recognized by the Ministry of Economy and Finance. Annual gross salary at least EUR 39,582 (as of August 2025).
programs.processing_time: Not specified; standard Talent Passport timelines apply
programs.notes: Fast-track within the Talent Passport framework specifically for innovative company employees. Issues 'Talent' residence permit valid up to 4 years. Family eligible.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Medical/Pharmacy Professional
programs.eligibility: Non-EU medical or pharmacy professionals with a license to practice in France. Annual salary at least EUR 41,386.48.
programs.notes: Part of the Talent Passport framework. Valid up to 4 years.

programs.name: Talent Passport - Person of International Renown
programs.eligibility: Non-EU nationals with established national or international renown in their field. Minimum resources: EUR 22,404.20.
programs.notes: Part of the Talent Passport framework. Valid up to 4 years. No minimum salary threshold, but proof of reputation required.

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Vanuatu removed from visa_free. Regulation (EU) 2025/11 (effective 3 February 2025) transferred Vanuatu from Annex II to Annex I (visa required). The prior record listed Vanuatu as visa-free with an 'unconfirmed post-suspension status' note — it is now confirmed visa-required.

CORRECTION: United Kingdom (GBR) added to visa_free. Post-Brexit UK citizens are listed in Annex II of Regulation 2018/1806-20250203 as confirmed in the current consolidated text. The prior record omitted the UK entirely.
CORRECTION: Hong Kong SAR (HKG) added to visa_free. Hong Kong SAR passport holders are listed in Annex II Part 2 of the consolidated Regulation. The prior record omitted HKG.
CORRECTION: Macao SAR (MAC) added to visa_free. Macao SAR (RAEM) passport holders are listed in Annex II Part 2 of the consolidated Regulation. The prior record omitted MAC.
CORRECTION: Timor-Leste (TLS) added to visa_free. Confirmed in Annex II Part 1; EU-Timor-Leste visa waiver agreement entered into force 1 May 2022. The prior record omitted Timor-Leste.

CORRECTION: Andorra duplicate entries consolidated. The prior record listed Andorra twice (once in a micro-state group with unlimited stay, once in Annex II with 90-day cap). Now consolidated into a single entry reflecting both the Annex II listing and the unlimited-stay/EES-exempt status.
CORRECTION: Monaco, San Marino, Holy See duplicates consolidated similarly. These were listed twice in the prior record.

CORRECTION: Nauru note updated to accurately reflect the Annex II footnote condition: exemption applies only 'from the date of entry into force of an agreement on visa exemption to be concluded with the EU.' No such agreement confirmed concluded as of June 2026.

CORRECTION: ETIAS eta entry updated: (a) count changed from '59 countries/territories' to 'approx. 61 countries/territories' reflecting addition of UK/HKG/MAC/TLS and removal of Vanuatu; (b) source_url updated to the April 2026 EC confirmation page; (c) EES operational status confirmed (10 April 2026).

CORRECTION: USA entry note updated to document the Annexe C exception for US diplomatic/service passport holders travelling for professional reasons (requires visa per Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 Annexe C).
VERIFIED: RBI minimum investment of EUR 300,000 confirmed current as of May 2026 via Business France official page.

VERIFIED: Bilateral diplomatic/service passport exemptions under Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 (Annexe B, in force 19 June 2025) carried forward unchanged — these could not be re-scraped from legifrance.gouv.fr in this validation pass but were confirmed via prior research.

VERIFIED: EU visa waiver agreement in-force dates added to notes for all bilateral bilateral Pacific/Caribbean entries (Dominica, Grenada, Kiribati, Marshall Islands, Micronesia, Palau, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent, Samoa, Solomon Islands, Timor-Leste, Tonga, Trinidad and Tobago, Tuvalu, UAE). Sources: EUR-Lex visa waiver agreements summary.
gaps: Nauru: No EU-Nauru visa waiver agreement found in EUR-Lex summary as of June 2026. Nauru is in Annex II but with an agreement-contingent footnote. Practical visa-free status is legally ambiguous; listed with a conditional note.

Congo (Brazzaville) diplomatic passport waiver: The Annexe B waiver stated expiry of '30 septembre 2024'. Not included in conditional_access pending confirmation of renewal or lapse from legifrance.gouv.fr — legifrance was not scrapeable in this pass.
France-Visas.gouv.fr returned HTTP 403 and could not be scraped directly for the full visa wizard nationality list in this validation pass.

Legifrance.gouv.fr Annexe B/C of the Arrêté du 10 mai 2010 (updated 19 June 2025) was not directly re-scraped in this validation pass. The diplomatic/service passport conditional_access entries are carried forward from prior research; the full list of countries covered by Annexe B should be re-verified against the 19 June 2025 version when legifrance becomes accessible.

ETIAS precise launch date and definitive count of eligible nationalities: official sources as of April 2026 confirm 'last quarter of 2026' but no specific date. Nationality count listed as 'approx. 61' pending official enumeration.
France's overseas territories (French Polynesia, New Caledonia, Wallis and Futuna, etc.) may have different visa rules; only metropolitan France / standard Schengen rules captured here.
Tonga EU visa waiver agreement status: listed as 'provisional application' since 2015. Formal entry into force date not confirmed from official sources in this pass.
UAR/UAE (ARE) EU visa waiver agreement: listed as 'provisional application' from 6 May 2015. Formal entry into force date not confirmed from official sources in this pass.

outbound_official.note: No single official French government outbound visa-list page located analogous to India's mea.gov.in/vffin. The French Ministry of Foreign Affairs (Ministère de l'Europe et des Affaires étrangères) publishes 'Conseils aux voyageurs' (Travel Advice) at https://www.diplomatie.gouv.fr/fr/conseils-aux-voyageurs as a per-country lookup tool, where each destination country page includes an 'Entrée / séjour' section describing entry requirements for French citizens. There is no consolidated page that groups all destinations by visa category (visa-free, visa on arrival, e-visa, ETA). The service-public.gouv.fr page https://www.service-public.gouv.fr/particuliers/vosdroits/F32833 only directs French citizens to consult individual destination pages. Rely on destination-side data and the per-country pages on diplomatie.gouv.fr for outbound requirements.
